title: Diggs

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer encounters some minor frustration (implied)
jokes.punchline: His signature exclamation of annoyance
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfectly integrated character comedy but routine by this point in the series

jokes.text: D'oh! Stupid fuse.
jokes.setup: Another minor household frustration
jokes.punchline: Homer's frustrated reaction to a blown fuse
jokes.explanation: Second D'oh in quick succession - diminishing returns on the same gag

jokes.text: Kingdom of Moab is the least interesting in the Bible. Amen. Amen! We're done!
jokes.setup: Reverend giving a boring sermon conclusion
jokes.punchline: Congregation's eager 'We're done!' response to mundane biblical fact
jokes.explanation: Good church satire - the congregation's relief at ending a dull sermon is relatable

jokes.text: Amen! We're done!
jokes.setup: Reverend ends his boring sermon
jokes.punchline: Homer's overly enthusiastic relief
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction - his impatience and lack of reverence in church setting

jokes.text: ill-advised mission in Indonesia
jokes.setup: Introducing guest speaker
jokes.punchline: Casually describing a mission as 'ill-advised'
jokes.explanation: Dry humor about missionary work - understated but effective

jokes.text: One boy, named Sumadi, never comes to church. He is always tying the monkeys' tails together and yelling, 'Tiger is gone,' when, in fact, tiger is there.
jokes.setup: Describing a troublesome village boy
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd pranks involving monkeys and false tiger warnings
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd - the detail about tigers being there makes it perfect

jokes.text: After all, we are all just children of Allah. I mean God. Sorry. Recent convert.
jokes.setup: Religious platitude in Christian church
jokes.punchline: Accidentally uses Allah, then awkwardly corrects himself as recent convert
jokes.explanation: Sharp religious satire with perfect awkward timing - the 'recent convert' makes it brilliant

jokes.text: Poor Sumadi. Born in such a terrible country.
jokes.setup: Homer responding to missionary's story
jokes.punchline: Casually dismissive comment about entire country while missing the real issue
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - well-meaning but completely missing the point with casual ignorance

jokes.text: Wasn't our sympathetic nodding enough?
jokes.setup: Second collection being announced
jokes.punchline: Homer's complaint about having to give actual money vs. just nodding
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor about performative charity - economical and relatable

jokes.text: I spent all I had lighting that candle for Great Aunt Betty.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining why she has no money
jokes.punchline: Implies she spent significant money on a single candle
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about church candle costs - functional but not memorable

jokes.text: I hope that wasn't somebody's Aunt Betty or something.
jokes.setup: Marge mentioning Great Aunt Betty
jokes.punchline: Bart's meta-awareness that he might be making fun of a real relative
jokes.explanation: Minor meta joke showing Bart's occasional self-awareness

jokes.text: Sorry, son, I only have 20s. And if God needs money, why doesn't he just write another Bible? The first one sold pretty well.
jokes.setup: Homer refusing to give money to church
jokes.punchline: Treating the Bible as a commercial publishing venture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - treating God like a commercial author needing to write sequels. Perfect character integration and subversive wit.

jokes.text: Does anyone remember when it was considered rude to talk in church? Pipe down, Ned.
jokes.setup: Ned complaining about talking in church
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately tells him to shut up, proving Ned's point
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - Homer immediately violates the rule Ned is complaining about

jokes.text: Pipe down, Ned.
jokes.setup: Ned complaining about talking in church
jokes.punchline: Homer telling him to be quiet, thus talking in church himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - Homer contributing to the problem while criticizing the critic

jokes.text: Life is good.
jokes.setup: Bart has money in his pocket
jokes.punchline: His simple contentment before everything goes wrong
jokes.explanation: Setup for what's coming - dramatic irony as audience knows trouble is ahead

jokes.text: Do you have my money? What? Do you have my money? How could I? Right, right, too soon.
jokes.setup: Homer immediately demanding repayment
jokes.punchline: Brief moment of self-awareness that it's too soon, then continuing anyway
jokes.explanation: Good escalating pressure gag showing Homer's lack of self-control

jokes.text: Seriously, do you have my money? Where's my money? Where's... my... mon... ey?
jokes.setup: Continuing money demands
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate and drawn-out delivery
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the money demands - getting increasingly manic

jokes.text: Did you know there really was a Duncan Hines?
jokes.setup: Bart offering a 'fun fact' to distract Homer
jokes.punchline: Completely random trivia about cake mix brand founder
jokes.explanation: Unexpected random fact as desperate distraction - absurd specificity works

jokes.text: Pressure's how you make your beloved diamonds, Marge.
jokes.setup: Marge telling Homer to quit badgering Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer's pseudo-scientific justification using diamond formation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer pseudo-wisdom - applying scientific concepts incorrectly to justify bad behavior

jokes.text: I don't have any diamonds.
jokes.setup: Homer's diamond pressure analogy
jokes.punchline: Marge's literal response deflating his metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - matter-of-fact deflation of Homer's grandiose analogy

jokes.text: I will eat anything for money! Except money!
jokes.setup: Diggs announcing he'll eat anything for money
jokes.punchline: Logical exception that money itself wouldn't be edible
jokes.explanation: Clever logical exception to the general statement - good wordplay

jokes.text: And here's something that'll make you forget about the other stuff. Ay caramba.
jokes.setup: Kids offering increasingly gross things to eat
jokes.punchline: Bart's signature exclamation suggests final item is particularly awful
jokes.explanation: Bart's catchphrase works as reaction to unseen gross object

jokes.text: 20 big ones says you're not messed-up enough to eat... this.
jokes.setup: High stakes bet being offered
jokes.punchline: Challenge to eat something presumably disgusting (dead frog revealed)
jokes.explanation: Standard gross-out setup - functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Do it, and you'll always be known as the kid who ate the dead frog.
jokes.setup: Encouraging Bart to take the bet
jokes.punchline: Framing permanent reputation damage as an incentive
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about how kids get stuck with awful reputations

jokes.text: Son, by rights, I should be playing banjo at your funeral.
jokes.setup: Doctor discussing Bart's condition after eating formaldehyde frog
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and cheerful reference to his own musical funeral performance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dr. Hibbert line - morbid content delivered with his characteristic cheer, banjo detail is perfect

jokes.text: Did you owe me some money?
jokes.setup: Bart giving Homer money
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete memory lapse about the loan he was obsessing over
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Homer's attention span - obsessive then completely forgotten

jokes.text: Ew! It's the kid who ate the dead frog!
jokes.setup: Kids seeing Bart at school
jokes.punchline: Immediate identification and rejection based on frog incident
jokes.explanation: Predictable follow-through on the reputation consequence - functional but expected

jokes.text: Herbivore! That's someone who eats plants. I named the frog Herb.
jokes.setup: Someone calling Bart a herbivore as an insult
jokes.punchline: Bart's correction reveals he named the frog, making the insult accidentally accurate
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that reveals Bart's unexpected sentimentality about naming the frog

jokes.text: I'm already the boy who shared a comb with a boy who threw up on the boy who wet his pants at the winter carnival.
jokes.setup: Milhouse explaining why he can't sit with Bart
jokes.punchline: Elaborate chain of social contamination through increasingly gross associations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating chain showing how school reputations work - perfectly structured buildup

jokes.text: What's the matter, frog in your throat?
jokes.setup: Bullies taunting Bart
jokes.punchline: Literal application of common idiom to his frog-eating incident
jokes.explanation: Solid pun connecting common expression to recent incident

jokes.text: All we hear is: ribbit, ribbit!
jokes.setup: Continuing the frog-eating taunts
jokes.punchline: Claiming Bart now only makes frog sounds
jokes.explanation: Weak follow-up to the frog joke - predictable and adds little

jokes.text: Oh, so you're the reason I had to dissect a Muppet in biology today.
jokes.setup: Diggs responding to Bart being 'the kid who ate the frog'
jokes.punchline: Implies school ran out of real frogs, had to use Muppets for dissection
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd escalation - dissecting Muppets is a perfect surreal consequence

jokes.text: Even after Springfield Montessori opened across the street?
jokes.setup: Diggs transferred to Springfield Elementary on purpose
jokes.punchline: Better school option available right across the street
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about school choice and Diggs' odd decision

jokes.text: That place is really in your face.
jokes.setup: Discussing Montessori school
jokes.punchline: Using 'in your face' to mean aggressive when Montessori is famously gentle
jokes.explanation: Ironic use of aggressive terminology for famously passive educational approach

jokes.text: I'm president, secretary treasurer and faculty advisor. That took a lot of fancy paperwork.
jokes.setup: Diggs explaining his falconry club leadership
jokes.punchline: Holding all positions including faculty advisor despite being a student
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic about bureaucratic workarounds - establishes Diggs' character

jokes.text: Habemus Papam! We have a poop! It's white. You're in.
jokes.setup: Testing Bart for falconry club membership
jokes.punchline: Papal announcement parody about bird droppings as membership test
jokes.explanation: Clever papal election parody applied to bird droppings test - unexpected highbrow/lowbrow mix

jokes.text: Can the Esperanto Society be far behind? I mean... Cu la Esperanto-Societo esti multe malantau?
jokes.setup: Commenting on revival of obscure clubs
jokes.punchline: Nerdy reference to constructed language, then actually speaking it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse nerdiness - knowing obscure constructed language and actually speaking it

jokes.text: Two languages, and somehow no one will talk to you. Gi estas vera. Mi tre soleca.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Milhouse's bilingual skills
jokes.punchline: Knowing multiple languages but still being friendless, then proving it by speaking Esperanto
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation about how intellectual pursuits don't guarantee social success

jokes.text: Oh, is it all right to say 'tarde'? Mmm, hey, you've said it twice, Marge-- you're in pretty deep.
jokes.setup: Marge worried about using Spanish word
jokes.punchline: Homer's mock-serious concern about her 'deep' involvement with Spanish
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about cultural anxiety over using foreign words

jokes.text: Wow, you don't know how impressive that is to a boy whose dad can't even get the dog to sit. Sit. Sit. Sit... Sit! I don't know what that is, but it's not sitting!
jokes.setup: Bart praising Diggs' bird training
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate failure to get Santa's Little Helper to sit, with increasingly frustrated commands
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast showing Homer's incompetence vs. Diggs' skill with animals

jokes.text: There comes a time when a boy sneaks his first beer. Finally! I was beginning to think that kid was a... teetotaler.
jokes.setup: Homer thinking Bart is drinking beer
jokes.punchline: His relief that Bart isn't a non-drinker, treating sobriety as the real problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting - worried about wrong things, treating teetotaler like an insult

jokes.text: I wasn't drinking. I was learning that nature isn't a complete waste of space.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining what he was actually doing
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment to nature as 'not a complete waste'
jokes.explanation: Good Bart line showing his grudging acceptance of nature appreciation

jokes.text: It's even better than I thought. You've found your Moe Szyslak!
jokes.setup: Bart describing his friendship with Diggs
jokes.punchline: Homer comparing it to his relationship with his alcoholic bartender
jokes.explanation: Homer projecting his own dysfunctional relationships onto Bart's friendship

jokes.text: In just a few short years, you'll be getting your version of baked on your version of pot listening to your version of Journey.
jokes.setup: Homer's excited prediction about Bart's future
jokes.punchline: Assuming Bart will follow his exact pattern of drug use and bad music
jokes.explanation: Homer's nostalgic projection of his own questionable youth onto Bart

jokes.text: Just goofing on you. This is the finger he bit off.
jokes.setup: Claiming bird only bit off one finger
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was joking, then showing a different missing finger
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - denying one claim while confirming another equally disturbing one

jokes.text: Yes, Bart, that is how you conjugate the verb 'to do.' Well done.
jokes.setup: Bart excitedly saying 'I did it! He did it! We did it!'
jokes.punchline: Diggs treating it as a grammar lesson rather than celebration
jokes.explanation: Shows Diggs' intellectual personality - finding grammar in moments of excitement

jokes.text: Give that back! All I got is a hole in my face!
jokes.setup: Falcon stealing Willie's earring
jokes.punchline: Willie's complaint about his piercing being reduced to 'hole in face'
jokes.explanation: Willie's crude description of his earring situation is appropriately character-driven

jokes.text: Nobody could communicate that. Except maybe Gerard Manley Hopkins.
jokes.setup: Discussing bird behavior
jokes.punchline: Obscure Victorian poet reference for communicating with birds
jokes.explanation: Perfectly establishes Diggs' intellectual character with obscure but relevant reference

jokes.text: I've already eaten.
jokes.setup: Diggs offering food to Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact response to being offered a mouse
jokes.explanation: Bart's casual response to being offered mouse food - good deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Say something so I'll know you're not hurt. I'm really hurt. Phew!
jokes.setup: Bart asking Diggs to confirm he's not injured after falling
jokes.punchline: Diggs saying he's hurt, but Bart interpreting it as confirmation he's fine
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke - Bart's misinterpretation of literal vs. emotional hurt

jokes.text: Well, my dad said don't bring home any more dogs, cats, turtles or snakes, but he never said anything about predatory birds.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he can keep the falcon at home
jokes.punchline: Finding loophole in parental pet prohibition
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic - finding technical loophole in parental rules

jokes.text: You Korean girls know what a soldier likes.
jokes.setup: Abe being fed while watching football
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate racial assumption about his caregiver
jokes.explanation: Typical Abe casual racism - establishes character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: No, I signed them myself. They're people I would've liked to come visit.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about visitors
jokes.punchline: Diggs admitting he faked his own visitor signatures
jokes.explanation: Touching and sad reveal about Diggs' loneliness disguised as casual admission

jokes.text: You're like Diogenes. But with slingshot in place of lamp.
jokes.setup: Bart asking for truth
jokes.punchline: Comparing Bart to ancient Greek philosopher seeking truth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - sophisticated classical reference perfectly adapted to Bart

jokes.text: This hospital has another doctor?
jokes.setup: Being told another doctor wants to see Diggs
jokes.punchline: Surprise that Springfield hospital has multiple doctors
jokes.explanation: Mild Springfield incompetence joke - functional but not standout

jokes.text: He's just riding the thermals from Dad's butt.
jokes.setup: Bird circling around the house
jokes.punchline: Explanation that it's using Homer's flatulence for lift
jokes.explanation: Good lowbrow humor connecting bird behavior to Homer's bodily functions

jokes.text: At least someone in this family is using them.
jokes.setup: Bart's comment about Homer's flatulence providing thermals
jokes.punchline: Lisa's practical observation about waste utilization
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa response - finding environmental efficiency in crude humor

jokes.text: This is one of those Arkham Asylum-type hospitals.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Bart can't visit Diggs
jokes.punchline: Comparing psychiatric hospital to fictional Batman asylum
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference to explain serious mental health facility

jokes.text: I have about eight of them.
jokes.setup: Bart asking what Homer knows about friends like Diggs
jokes.punchline: Homer claiming to have multiple mentally ill friends
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual admission about his social circle - darkly funny

jokes.text: You hug the bedpost. Then I hug the bedpost. That way we never hug each other.
jokes.setup: Offering Bart comfort
jokes.punchline: Elaborate system to avoid direct physical affection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - wants to be supportive but emotionally avoidant, creates absurd workaround

jokes.text: Yeah, and a rubber perch! Yeah, yeah, and a... I get it. It sucks. Really sucks. Rubber birdfeeder.
jokes.setup: Bullies making fun of Diggs being institutionalized
jokes.punchline: Running out of rubber psychiatric facility jokes
jokes.explanation: Shows bullies running out of material for their cruel jokes

jokes.text: Making fun of the mentally ill-- real classy. But you guys were... We were what, Captain Sensitive?
jokes.setup: Bart calling out the bullies
jokes.punchline: Bullies turning his compassion into an insult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's growth and bullies' deflection

jokes.text: The rumors of my bonkertude have been greatly exaggerated.
jokes.setup: Diggs appearing unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Mark Twain quote parody using made-up word 'bonkertude'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Diggs line - sophisticated literary reference with playful neologism

jokes.text: DSM-5 indicates paranoid schizophrenia, but that work is mired in controversy. Mired.
jokes.setup: Discussing his diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Academic discussion of psychiatric manual followed by emphasis on word choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Diggs - clinical knowledge combined with word nerdiness

jokes.text: I'm fine. My meds won't wear off till tonight. Then I'll have Freedom tear you to shreds.
jokes.setup: Reassuring Bart he's safe
jokes.punchline: Casual threat about what happens when medication wears off
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about medication timing and bird attacks

jokes.text: You're discounting all talk radio.
jokes.setup: Bart saying crazy people pretending to be crazy isn't funny
jokes.punchline: Pointing out that's the entire talk radio format
jokes.explanation: Sharp media satire delivered with perfect deadpan timing

jokes.text: Why am I not surprised the only sport you do is one where a bird brings you food?
jokes.setup: Commenting on falconry competition
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic observation about passive nature of the sport
jokes.explanation: Typical Comic Book Guy snark about others' activities

jokes.text: Um, a falcon's greatest joy is to serve its master. Fatso!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy's noble statement about falcons
jokes.punchline: Bird immediately insults him
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - bird immediately contradicts the claim about servitude

jokes.text: Once again, I must point out that you are not a parrot. Fatso, fatso! Pees in the shower!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy correcting the bird
jokes.punchline: Bird continuing with personal insults
jokes.explanation: Escalation of bird insults with embarrassing personal details

jokes.text: Um... with our minds? No. With this rope. I'm a messed-up kid. I'm not Magneto.
jokes.setup: Diggs planning to open all bird cages
jokes.punchline: Bart's realistic assessment of his lack of mutant powers
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware joke about Bart's limitations vs. comic book heroes

jokes.text: If you see the stork what brings us babies, kill it!
jokes.setup: All the birds being released from their cages
jokes.punchline: Homer's specific request to murder the stork
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - turning a beautiful scene into crude anti-reproductive sentiment

jokes.text: If you see the stork what brings us babies, kill it!
jokes.setup: Birds being released from cages
jokes.punchline: Moe's instruction to murder the stork to prevent more babies
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe darkness - wanting to stop baby delivery at the source

jokes.text: Well, I guess some delicate birds are safer in a cage.
jokes.setup: Diggs being taken back to hospital
jokes.punchline: Bart's metaphorical understanding about mental health care
jokes.explanation: Touching and mature realization about mental health treatment disguised as bird metaphor

jokes.text: I had thought of it as a therapeutic milieu, but now 'cage' is stuck in my head, so thanks.
jokes.setup: Bart's cage metaphor
jokes.punchline: Diggs' clinical language being corrupted by Bart's simpler metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Diggs response - intellectual terminology replaced by Bart's blunt imagery

jokes.text: She came in on a Saturday. Missed her kid's karate demonstration. He was going up a belt.
jokes.setup: Explaining therapist helped him understand his feelings
jokes.punchline: Excessive detail about therapist's sacrifice and her child's achievement
jokes.explanation: Shows Diggs' tendency to over-explain and consider everyone's perspective

jokes.text: Mom, you didn't cook the falcon? Of course not. It's just duck.
jokes.setup: Family dinner after falcon release
jokes.punchline: Bart's relief followed by Marge serving similar bird
jokes.explanation: Mild ironic humor about eating different bird species

tmdb_overview: A recent transfer student named Diggs rescues Bart from a confrontation with bullies.
